channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: Lukashenko Says Belarus Poses No Military Threat to Ukraine, Apologizes to Zelensky
item.description: Belarusian leader Alexander Lukashenko stated Belarus poses no military threat to Ukraine and apologized to President Zelensky for previous harsh remarks, a notable diplomatic signal as G7 leaders discuss pathways to ending the Russia-Ukraine war.

item.title: G7 Summit Opens With Focus on Ukraine War and Trump Iran Deal
item.description: Group of Seven leaders gathered in Évian-les-Bains for their first full day of meetings, with Russia war in Ukraine and the tentative US-Iran peace agreement dominating the agenda as Zelensky joins discussions.

item.title: Netanyahu Minister Defies US-Iran MoU, Vows Lebanon Occupation Until Hezbollah Disarmed
item.description: A senior Israeli minister rejected the newly announced US-Iran memorandum of understanding, vowing that Israeli military operations in southern Lebanon will continue until Hezbollah is fully disarmed — a direct challenge to the peace framework.

item.title: Iran Says End of War Must Include End of Israeli Occupation in Lebanon
item.description: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared that the permanent end to the war is inseparable from ending Israeli occupation in Lebanon, asserting that the US-Iran MOU covers all fronts including Lebanon — contradicting Israel interpretation.

item.title: Ukrainian Drones Strike Moscow Refinery and Krasnodar Oil Depot in Massive Overnight Raid
item.description: Ukrainian long-range drones hit the Gazpromneft Moscow oil refinery just 15 km from the Kremlin and an oil depot in the Krasnodar region near Crimea, sparking major fires and damaging fuel infrastructure as Zelensky calls the strikes coercion for Russia to end the war.

item.title: US Air Force B-52 bomber crashes at Edwards AFB, all 8 crew killed
item.description: A B-52 Stratofortress crashed shortly after takeoff at Edwards Air Force Base in California, bursting into flames and killing all eight crew members aboard, including two Boeing employees.

item.title: G7 leaders open summit talks on Ukraine and Middle East as Zelenskyy joins in France
item.description: Leaders of the Group of Seven opened their first full day of meetings in Evian-les-Bains, with a packed agenda covering the war in Ukraine and the tentative US-Iran peace deal, as Ukrainian President Zelenskyy arrived for talks.

item.title: Trump meets G7 leaders and Zelenskyy after US-Iran agreement, Ukraine in focus
item.description: President Trump held meetings with G7 leaders and Ukrainian President Zelenskyy at the summit in France, with discussions centering on the tentative Iran peace deal and shifting attention toward resolving the Russia-Ukraine war.

item.title: Oil prices retreat to $80 a barrel after US-Iran peace framework deal
item.description: Crude prices tumbled toward $80 a barrel after the US and Iran agreed to a tentative peace framework, as traders anticipated the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and resumption of Middle East oil flows.

item.title: Iran soccer team blocked from training in US, welcomed by Tijuana amid World Cup
item.description: Iran's World Cup squad was denied entry to train in the United States amid the recent conflict, but the Mexican border city of Tijuana rolled out a welcome for the team caught between sport and geopolitics.

item.title: Netanyahu and Trump on Collision Course as US-Iran Deal Falls Short of Israeli War Goals
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu pushed back against the US-Iran peace agreement, declaring that "the struggle has not ended." The deal falls short of Israel's stated war objectives, creating a rift between the two allies at a critical moment.

item.title: Historic Kyiv Pechersk Lavra Monastery Set Ablaze in Russian Strike
item.description: A nearly 1,000-year-old monastery — one of Ukraine's most sacred cultural sites with golden domes towering over Kyiv for centuries — was set ablaze during a Russian attack. Firefighters worked to contain the damage to the historic cathedral famed for its frescoes and silver artefacts.

item.title: Ukraine drives rush for mini drone laser-targeting systems as battlefield demand surges
item.description: Defense companies are racing to miniaturize laser target-marking technology for small UAVs after Ukraine requested compact systems suited to its drone-heavy battlefield tactics, spurring new product launches across the industry.

item.title: China develops military stealth coating that could be mass-produced as cheap house paint
item.description: Chinese researchers have created a new microwave-absorbing material that could make stealth technology far cheaper to produce, sparking debate over reports that commercial stealth paint may already be available on the open market.

item.title: Central banks accelerate gold buying as de-dollarization trend deepens worldwide
item.description: The World Gold Council reports that nearly 90% of central banks plan to increase gold holdings as countries diversify reserves away from the US dollar, reflecting a structural shift in global finance amid geopolitical fragmentation.

item.title: Congress reacts with skepticism to Trump-Iran deal, demands briefing on secret terms
item.description: Republican and Democratic senators alike expressed deep skepticism about the US-Iran agreement, with GOP hawks calling it a surrender and Democrats demanding immediate classified briefings on the deal’s undisclosed terms.

item.title: JD Vance admits unresolved issues remain in Iran peace deal ahead of G7 talks
item.description: Vice President JD Vance acknowledged that several issues still remain in the US-Iran peace agreement as G7 leaders prepare to discuss the Middle East, tempering expectations for a finalized deal at the summit.

item.title: Surging Chinese exports threaten European economy, raising alarm at G7 summit
item.description: Eight years of US tariffs have failed to dent China industrial output, and a new wave of cheap exports is now pressuring European manufacturers, forcing G7 leaders to confront an escalating trade crisis.

item.title: ECB chief Lagarde welcomes Iran ceasefire but warns inflation may persist
item.description: ECB President Christine Lagarde called the US-Iran ceasefire good news that could help reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but several ECB colleagues cautioned it would not immediately bring down elevated euro zone inflation.

item.title: Norway Crown Princess son convicted of rape, sentenced to four years
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby, eldest son of Norway Crown Princess Mette-Marit, was found guilty of two counts of rape and sentenced to four years in prison in a case that has shaken the Norwegian royal family.

item.title: Trump Iran deal offers exit from war but raises fresh political risks at home
item.description: The US-Iran peace agreement gives Trump a way out of the 3.5-month conflict, but skeptical conservatives in Congress are raising concerns over whether Iran will regain access to its frozen assets under the deal.

item.title: Takaichi pushes mineral stockpiling plan at G7, seeks to bridge divides as Japan’s Iron Lady takes the stage
item.description: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi proposed a joint critical minerals stockpiling initiative at the G7 summit to counter China’s dominance, while also welcoming the US-Iran memorandum as a step toward regional de-escalation.

item.title: South Korea and Indonesia rein in derivatives trading to defend currencies amid energy shock
item.description: Asian economies heavily reliant on imported energy are tightening oversight of speculative currency derivatives trades as the Iran war-driven oil disruption rattles forex markets.

item.title: Ukrainian drone attack sets oil depot ablaze in Russia’s Krasnodar region near Crimea
item.description: A drone attack sparked a major fire at an oil depot in the village of Poltavskaya in Russia’s southern Krasnodar region, forcing the closure of a road connecting to annexed Crimea. The main fire was later extinguished.

item.title: European leaders warn Trump on Iran deal risks, push Ukraine rethink at G7 summit
item.description: European leaders told Trump at the G7 summit in Evian that a superficial interim Iran deal risks entrenching Tehran's nuclear and missile programs, while pressing him to rethink his Ukraine strategy and stand behind Kyiv.
